description: Platform hardening and feature expansion: migrate off OpenAI, add timeouts, streaming, templates, scheduling, cost tracking, and more

userStories.title: Add utility_completion helper with fallback chain to llm_client
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a utility_completion() function that tries Gemini Flash, then DeepSeek, then gpt-5-mini so cheap utility calls don't depend on OpenAI.
acceptanceCriteria: New constant UTILITY_MODEL_CHAIN = ['google/gemini-2.5-flash', 'deepseek/deepseek-chat', 'openai/gpt-5-mini'] in llm_client.py
New async function utility_completion(messages, **kwargs) -> str that iterates the chain, skipping models without API keys, and calling chat_completion for the first available
If a model errors at runtime, catches the exception and tries the next model in the chain
If all models fail, raises the last exception
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Migrate all hardcoded gpt-5-mini calls to utility_completion
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all cheap LLM calls routed through utility_completion so I can run the system without an OpenAI key.
acceptanceCriteria: executor.py _triage_failure uses utility_completion instead of chat_completion('gpt-5-mini', ...)
executor.py _compress_for_handoff uses utility_completion
agent_skills.py: generate_skill_from_success, generate_skill_from_failure, generate_correction_skill, _refine_description, extract_concepts_from_skill all use utility_completion
agent_memory.py generate_and_save_lesson uses utility_completion
actions.py _generate_title uses utility_completion instead of raw AsyncOpenAI
No remaining hardcoded 'gpt-5-mini' string literals outside of the UTILITY_MODEL_CHAIN definition
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update DEFAULT_MODELS_BY_AGENT_TYPE to prefer Anthropic/Gemini
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want agent defaults to use Anthropic and Gemini models instead of OpenAI.
acceptanceCriteria: DEFAULT_MODELS_BY_AGENT_TYPE updated: code_execution -> anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6, data_retrieval -> google/gemini-2.5-flash, spreadsheet -> anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6, coding -> anthropic/claude-opus-4-6, general -> anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6, mcp -> anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6
FALLBACK_MODEL changed from openai/gpt-5 to anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6
get_default_model_for_agent fallback logic tries Anthropic models before Gemini before OpenAI when iterating registry
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Migrate planner to use chat_completion with Claude Opus
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want the planner to use Claude Opus via chat_completion instead of hardcoding AsyncOpenAI.
acceptanceCriteria: planner.py no longer imports or instantiates AsyncOpenAI directly
planner.py plan_tasks() calls chat_completion('claude-opus-4-6', messages, tools=[plan_tasks_tool]) where plan_tasks_tool schema matches PlannerOutput
For Anthropic: structured output achieved via tool_use — define a tool called plan_tasks whose input_schema is the PlannerOutput JSON schema, then parse the tool call arguments as PlannerOutput
chat_completion in llm_client.py gains optional tools parameter — for Anthropic, passes as tools param to client.messages.create; for OpenAI, uses response_format or tools as appropriate
Fallback chain for planning: tries claude-opus-4-6, then claude-sonnet-4-6, then gemini-2.5-pro, then gpt-5 (checks API key availability)
recovery_planner.py also migrated to use chat_completion with Claude Opus and tool_use
Existing planner system prompt preserved unchanged
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the most critical migration. The key challenge is translating OpenAI's response_format=PlannerOutput to Anthropic's tool_use pattern.

userStories.title: Update planner config dashboard for multi-provider models
userStories.description: As a user, I want the planner config page model dropdown to show models from all providers, not just OpenAI.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /planner-config response includes available_models field listing all models with configured API keys
/planner page model dropdown populated from available_models, grouped by provider
Default selection is claude-opus-4-6 if Anthropic key is present
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add chat_completion_stream to llm_client
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a streaming version of chat_completion that yields token chunks for real-time display.
acceptanceCriteria: New async generator function chat_completion_stream(model, messages, **kwargs) -> AsyncGenerator[str, None] in llm_client.py
Anthropic streaming via client.messages.stream() context manager, yielding text deltas from content_block_delta events
OpenAI-compatible streaming via AsyncOpenAI with stream=True, yielding delta.content chunks
Returns the same provider routing logic as chat_completion (anthropic vs openai-compatible)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add streaming SSE event and BaseAgent.supports_streaming flag

acceptanceCriteria: BaseAgent gains class attribute supports_streaming = False
New SSE event type task.llm_chunk with payload { task_id, chunk, model }
event_bus.publish called for each chunk when an agent uses streaming (implementation in executor _run_task or agent code)
sse.ts registers task.llm_chunk event type
use-action-events.ts handles task.llm_chunk — appends chunk text to a per-task streaming buffer in Zustand store (new field: streamingText in task override)

userStories.title: Wrap agent execution with asyncio.wait_for timeout
userStories.description: As a user, I want hung tasks to auto-fail after their timeout expires.
acceptanceCriteria: In _run_task, timeout resolved as: task.timeout_seconds > AGENT_TIMEOUTS[agent_type] > 300
Agent execution wrapped in asyncio.wait_for(agent.execute(...), timeout=resolved_timeout)
On asyncio.TimeoutError: task marked failed with error 'Task timed out after {N} seconds'
task.failed SSE event payload includes timeout: true when timeout caused the failure
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add timeout UI to TaskCard and TaskCardEditor

acceptanceCriteria: TaskCard shows 'Timed out' badge (clock icon + text) instead of generic 'Failed' when task.failed event had timeout: true

userStories.title: Add DELETE /actions/:id with cascade cleanup

Sub-actions (children with parent_action_id == action_id) recursively deleted first

userStories.title: Fix N+1 query in list_actions and add cursor-based pagination

search filters: WHERE title LIKE %search% OR root_prompt LIKE %search% (case-insensitive)
Cursor pagination: WHERE updated_at < cursor ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT limit+1, return next_cursor if more rows exist
Response schema updated: { actions: [...], next_cursor: str | null }

userStories.title: Add event IDs and ring buffer to EventBus for SSE replay
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the event bus to support event IDs and replay so clients can recover from disconnects.
acceptanceCriteria: EventBus gains per-action monotonic counter (_event_counters: dict[str, int])
Each published event gets an id field from the counter
EventBus keeps a ring buffer per action using collections.deque(maxlen=100) (_event_history: dict[str, deque])
New method replay_from(action_id, last_event_id) -> list returns events with id > last_event_id from the ring buffer
Ring buffer cleared when action completes or is deleted
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add bounded queues to EventBus
userStories.description: As a developer, I want event bus queues bounded so slow consumers don't cause memory growth.
acceptanceCriteria: EventBus.subscribe() creates asyncio.Queue(maxsize=500)
On QueueFull during publish: evict oldest event via queue.get_nowait() then put_nowait the new event, log a warning
Remove the existing dead-code QueueFull warning path that can never trigger on unbounded queues
New method queue_depth(action_id) -> dict[str, int] returns current depth per subscriber queue
Log warning when any queue exceeds 400 events (80% capacity)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add SSE reconnect with exponential backoff on frontend
userStories.description: As a user, I want the SSE connection to auto-recover from network blips.
acceptanceCriteria: sse.ts: on EventSource.onerror, close connection and reconnect with exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 30s cap
Reset backoff timer on successful onopen
Browser automatically sends Last-Event-ID header on reconnect (built-in EventSource behavior)
action_events endpoint in actions.py reads Last-Event-ID from request headers, replays missed events from ring buffer before entering live loop
Remove the 3-errors-and-give-up logic from use-action-events.ts

Register all missing event types in sse.ts: task.recovering, task.recovered, action.replanning, action.retrying, sub_action.progress, task.acquisition, iteration.started, iteration.reasoning, iteration.tool_call, iteration.tool_result, iteration.completed, iteration.file_diff, iteration.terminal, task.recovery.started, task.recovery.attempt, task.recovery.exhausted, task.paused, task.resumed, task.user_guidance
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add reconnecting indicator to frontend

acceptanceCriteria: Zustand store gains sseConnected boolean field per action
sse.ts sets sseConnected=false on error, true on open

userStories.title: Add cost tracking to chat_completion and chat_completion_stream

userStories.title: Add ActionSchedule model and scheduler background task

userStories.title: Add ArtifactVersion model and versioning logic

Imported in models/__init__.py and database.py init_db()
